steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a problem — Cristall was "largely overlooked" by twentieth-century scholars — then pivots to a 2020 analysis that "helps illuminate" her contributions. The blank must introduce that second sentence with a transition that signals the analysis responds to and corrects the neglect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Illustrating this trend,’: Says the 2020 analysis illustrates the trend of overlooking Cristall, but the analysis does the opposite — it counters the neglect. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In accordance with these scholars,’: Implies the 2020 analysis agrees with the scholars who ignored Cristall, which contradicts what the sentence says. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In addition to this research,’: Frames the analysis as simply adding to prior scholarship rather than correcting an oversight. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Addressing this oversight,’: "Oversight" directly names the scholars' neglect, and "addressing" signals the analysis corrects it — a precise problem-to-solution transition. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
